column1: Story ID
column4: Priority

column1: US01
I want to create my applicant profile
So that I can prepare for applying for available jobs."
column4: "1. The applicant can enter personal and academic information including name

column1: 2. Required fields must be completed before saving.
3. The profile can be saved and reopened later.
4. An error message is shown if required fields are missing."
US02
I want to edit my applicant profile
So that my information remains accurate before or after submitting an application."
column4: "1. The applicant can update saved profile information.

column1: 2. Updated information is saved correctly.
3. Existing data is not lost unexpectedly."
column3: Profile maintenance after first draft submission.

column1: US03
I want to upload my CV
So that I can complete the application requirements for job applications."
column4: "1. The applicant can upload a CV file from local storage.

column1: 2. Only PDF and DOC files are accepted.
3. The uploaded file name is displayed after a successful upload.
4. The applicant can replace the uploaded CV with a new version.
5. An error message is shown if no file is selected or the file format is invalid."
US04
I want to browse available jobs
So that I can identify suitable opportunities before applying."
column4: "1. The system displays a list of open jobs.

column1: 2. Each job card shows at least the title
column4: deadline

column1: 3. Closed jobs are not shown in the default list."
column3: Core browsing function in pre-application stage.

column1: US05
I want to filter and search jobs
So that I can find suitable jobs more efficiently."
column4: "1. The applicant can filter jobs by module or activity

column1: 2. Filtered results update correctly.
3. An empty-state message is shown if no result matches."
US06
I want to view detailed job information
So that I can decide whether I should apply."
column4: "1. The applicant can open a job detail page from the job list.

column1: 2. Job details include description
column4: deadline

column1: 3. The page clearly shows whether the job is open or closed.
4. Required skills and knowledge are clearly displayed before the applicant applies."
US07
I want to submit an application for a job
So that I can be considered for the position."
column4: "1. The applicant must complete profile information before applying.

column1: 2. The applicant must upload a CV before applying.
3. An applicant can apply for at most three jobs.
4. An applicant cannot apply to the same job twice.
5. An applicant cannot apply to a closed job or after the deadline.
6. The system confirms successful submission.
7. The submitted application is recorded in the applicant's application list.
8. The MO can view the submitted application."
US08
I want to view the status of my submitted applications
So that I know whether my application is under review
column4: or selected."
column7: "1. The applicant can open a 'My Applications' page.

column1: 2. Each application shows job title
column4: and current status.

column1: 3. Status labels
4. The applicant can access related job details from the application record."
US09
I want to withdraw my application before a decision is made
So that I can stop being considered if my situation changes."
column4: "1. The applicant can withdraw an application only when its status is still pending or under review.

column1: 2. Withdrawn applications are clearly marked.
3. The MO can see that the application was withdrawn."
US10
column2: View Selection Result and Assignment Details

column1: I want to see that I have been selected and view the related assignment details
So that I know what role I have been assigned to."
column4: "1. The selected applicant can see the updated status as 'Selected'.

column1: 2. The assigned job details are clearly displayed.
3. The selected TA can view module or activity name
US11
I want to confirm that I have seen my selection result
So that the system can record my acknowledgement."
column4: "1. The selected TA can open the assigned job record and acknowledge the result.

column1: 2. The system records the acknowledgement status.
3. A confirmation message is shown after acknowledgement."
US12
column4: I want to create and publish a job post so that qualified students can apply for available TA opportunities."
column7: "1. The MO can enter job title

column1: 2. Required fields must be completed before publishing.
3. Published jobs appear in the TA applicant job list.
4. The MO can save the job as a draft or publish it directly."
US13
column4: I want to edit or close a job post so that outdated or filled jobs are no longer available for new applications."
column7: "1. The MO can edit job details before the deadline.

column1: 2. The MO can close a job post manually.
3. Closed jobs are marked as closed and cannot receive new applications."
US14
column4: I want to view applicants for a specific job so that I can review candidates efficiently."
column7: "1. The MO can open a list of applicants for each posted job.

column1: 2. Each applicant entry shows name
column4: and application status.

column1: 3. The applicant count is displayed clearly for the job."
US15
column4: I want to review an applicant's full profile and CV so that I can make an informed selection decision."
column7: "1. The MO can open an applicant detail page from the applicant list.

column1: 2. The page shows profile information and CV access.
3. The MO can return to the applicant list without losing context."
US16
column4: I want to view all jobs I have posted so that I can manage them efficiently."
column7: "1. The MO can open a page showing all jobs they have created.

column1: 2. Each job record shows at least the title
column4: and applicant count.

column1: 3. The MO can open a specific job from the list to edit it or review applicants.
4. Open
column3: and closed jobs are clearly distinguishable."
column5: "Provides the management entry point for edit

column1: US17
column4: I want to mark applicants as selected
column6: or waitlisted so that the recruitment outcome is recorded clearly."
column9: "1. The MO can set the application outcome to Selected

column1: 2. The updated decision is reflected in the applicant list.
3. The TA applicant sees the updated status in My Applications.
4. The system prevents selecting a TA for more than three jobs.
5. The MO is warned if the TA has reached the workload limit."
US18
column4: I want to view each TA's overall workload so that I can monitor allocation fairness and identify over-allocation risks."
column7: "1. The admin can view a list of selected TAs with workload summary.

column1: 2. The summary includes TA name
3. Workload is calculated based on selected jobs only.
4. Potentially overloaded TAs are easy to identify."
US19
column4: I want to filter workload records so that I can inspect specific TAs or overloaded cases more efficiently."
column7: "1. The admin can search by TA name or student ID.

column1: 2. The admin can filter by workload status.
3. An empty-state message is shown if no record matches the filter."
US20
column4: I want to see workload alerts for selected TAs so that I can quickly identify fairness or over-allocation issues."
column7: "1. The system flags selected TAs whose workload exceeds a clearly defined threshold.

column1: 2. Alerted records are visually distinguishable in the workload view.
3. The admin can inspect the related selected assignments for the flagged TA."
US21
column4: I want to log into the system so that I can access the functions available to my role."
column7: "1. A user can enter valid login credentials and access the system successfully.

column1: 2. An error message is shown if the credentials are invalid.
3. After login
column2: the user is directed to the correct role-specific page or dashboard.

column1: 4. The system keeps the user logged in during the current session."
column3: Core website entry point; required before role-specific functions can be used.

column1: US22
column4: I want to log out of the system so that my account is protected when I finish using it."
column7: "1. A logged-in user can click a logout option to end the session.

column1: 2. After logout
column2: the user is redirected to the login page.

column1: 3. Protected pages cannot be accessed directly after logout.
4. A clear message is shown when logout is completed successfully."
column3: Basic account security and session control.
